content:
- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, then resolved blocker status against completed task entries.
- Confirmed there are many unblocked failing bug tasks now that story-greenfield-cucumber-ha-harness 01-04 all pass true.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/bugs/docker-socket-permission-blocks-greenfield-ha-harness-runtime-validation.md because it is an unblocked failing bug with explicit high priority, which outranks the other failing bugs with no priority tag.

- Started by reading the progress log and current Ralph task pointer.
- Current task is docker-socket-permission-blocks-greenfield-ha-harness-runtime-validation.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to decide whether I must deeply review the plan or directly execute it if NOW EXECUTE is already present.

- The bug task file has no plan and no NOW EXECUTE marker; it ends at acceptance criteria.
- I need to perform the requested deep skeptical plan review, then write a concrete execution plan into the task file and append NOW EXECUTE at the end.
- Before editing, I am checking how other Ralph task files represent plans so I stay consistent with repo conventions.

- Live environment check disproved the original symptom: docker info succeeds, current user is in group docker, and /var/run/docker.sock is group-writable.
- Repo docs currently mention Docker daemon reachability, but they do not mention socket-permission/group membership as a prerequisite or likely failure mode.
- I am checking harness and Makefile error surfaces now to decide whether this bug closes as stale-only or whether a clearer source-controlled preflight/documentation fix is still warranted.

- Wrote the reviewed plan into the bug task file and switched it into NOW EXECUTE mode.
- Execution path is now: verify the focused HA wrapper again, then improve Docker preflight diagnostics and contributor-facing docs for socket permission failures.
- I am reading the k2-docs-loop skill before touching docs, per repo instructions.

- Implemented two direct diagnostic improvements: a new tools/docker/check-daemon.sh preflight for Make targets, and a harness-side hint appended on Docker socket permission errors.
- I noticed I accidentally introduced an explicit panic in a Rust test and am removing it immediately to stay within repo lint rules.
- Next I will run the k2-docs-loop skill against docs/src/how-to/run-tests.md, using the known target page instead of choose-doc because this task already pinpoints the docs area to update.

- The k2-docs-loop skill ran but K2 returned an unusable tool-call artifact instead of a markdown revision for run-tests.md.
- I fell back to a minimal manual docs edit after using the required skill, limiting the change to Docker socket access prerequisites and troubleshooting for permission denied failures.
- Next I am checking workspace status, cleaning any unwanted draft artifacts, then formatting and validating the code changes before the full make gates.

- make test-long failed after I added helper tests inside shared cucumber support; those tests were duplicated into every HA feature binary and changed ultra-long scheduling.
- I removed the duplicated tests to restore the suite surface to baseline while keeping the actual runtime diagnostic logic.
- Next I am cleaning lingering compose projects and rerunning make test-long from the cleaner baseline.

- The rerun of make test-long still hit unrelated HA scenario failures even after removing the duplicated support tests: full_cluster_outage_restore_quorum_then_converge and clone_failure_recovers_after_blocker_removed.
- This points to independent ultra-long suite instability on current HEAD rather than a regression in the Docker access change.
- I am killing the broad run again and switching to focused reruns of those exact wrappers so I can prove whether the failures are isolated flakes or deterministic scenario breakage.
